Problem Description We received an RFP from Xactly where this question was asked by the prospect. Solution Given Gift values and point ranges vary based on reward type and client configuration. Monetary gift cards and merchandise typically start at $5 (or the local currency equivalent) and can exceed $1,000. Points-based rewards are fully customizable by the client, with common configurations ranging from 0 to over 100,000 points, usually mapped to local currency or SOLI-adjusted equivalents. Experiential and premium rewards are available upon specific company requests. Award values are also client-specific, defined in fixed denominations and mapped to local currencies for each country. chunks.text: SF-079 wipro error message PACE To Product Closed High Complexity Nilimpa Solution Request 79 - wipro **Date:** 2025-07-23 | **Status:** To Product Closed | **Complexity:** High Complexity Subject Solution request 8579::Update default error message for WIPRO Brief error message update Problem Description Update default error message Solution Given When a Nominator initiates a group or single-user nomination, the system must validate the selected award and denomination against the configured points rules. Upon clicking "Next," in addition to existing checks, a points validation is triggered. If any validation fails, the system will display a clear and specific error message, and the Nominator must resolve the issue by either removing the affected user(s) or adjusting the points. The nomination cannot proceed until all validation errors are resolved. This validation logic is a global change and applies consistently across all clients and users on the platform. chunks.text: SF-270 Tata Technologies Automated override of existing data during new data push Unknown Not Set Nilimpa Solution Request 270 - Tata Technologies **Date:** 2026-06-11 | **Status:** Unknown | **Complexity:** Not Set Brief Possibility to check if existing data can be overridden once new active data is pushed via CSV upload. Problem Description Hi team, For Tata Technologies (Company ID - 1102), employee data was previously being pushed via SFTP. However, due to issues on the client's end, they have now switched to CSV uploads for employee data updates. Currently, the client uploads only the active associates through the CSV files. As a result, associates who have become inactive are not included in the latest uploads but continue to remain active in our system. This is causing discrepancies, as inactive associates are still appearing in the Active Employees Data Report. We would like to check the feasibility of implementing the following solution: Once a new CSV file is uploaded, the existing employee data should be automatically reconciled with the latest file. Any associate record that is not present in the latest CSV upload should be automatically marked as inactive in the system. Could you please review this requirement and let us know the best possible approach to address this scen
